description: Assign motor IDs to leader and/or follower arm
explanation: Sets Dynamixel motor IDs on the arm. Solo Tech arms come pre-configured — skip unless you see a 'missing motor ids' error. Interactive: detects ports by prompting unplug/replug. RealMan uses network, not USB.
expected_output: Motor IDs set successfully. Proceed to calibration.

validation.rule: Success message must appear; no 'missing motor ids' error on subsequent calibration

common_errors: missing motor ids — this is exactly when to run this command
Wrong port detected — follow unplug/replug prompts carefully
Multiple ports detected — choose correct one from the displayed list

description: Calibrate joint limits and zero positions for arms
explanation: Teaches the software the physical limits and zero positions of each arm so commanded angles match real hardware. Required after motor ID setup or if arm was moved while powered off.

validation.rule: Calibration config saved in ~/.solo/. Subsequent teleop should not error on calibration

common_errors: Arm moved while powered off — recalibrate
Single arm failure — you can rerun calibration for just that arm
Ensure workspace is unobstructed and follow all on-screen prompts

description: List motors visible on serial ports (diagnostic)
explanation: Non-destructive scan to see which motor IDs respond on connected serial ports. Use for troubleshooting connection/motor sync issues.
expected_output: List of detected motor IDs and ports

validation.rule: Output lists motor IDs; no error on port open

common_errors: No motors found — check USB cable and power (12V follower / 5V leader on SO arms)
Permission denied on /dev/tty — run solo setup-usb on Linux

description: Run deep diagnostics on arm connection and motors
explanation: Deeper diagnostic pass — checks serial communication, motor health, and sync state. Use after scan if scan shows partial results.

validation.rule: Report shows no critical errors for connected arms

common_errors: failed to sync — faulty board, loose power cable, or detached 3-pin motor cable
failed to read motor ids — check daisy chain integrity and USB cable
